title: Harnessing the Power of Renewable Energy
passage:
The Future of Renewable Energy

As the world grapples with climate change, renewable energy has gained significant attention. Solar, wind, and hydroelectric power are becoming increasingly popular alternatives to fossil fuels. Governments and organizations are investing heavily in these technologies to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and promote sustainable development. Solar panels, for instance, can convert sunlight into electricity, providing a clean and abundant energy source. Wind turbines harness the power of the wind to generate electricity, while hydroelectric plants use flowing water to produce energy. Each of these methods presents unique benefits and challenges. For example, solar energy is available in most regions, but its efficiency can be affected by weather conditions. On the other hand, wind energy is most effective in areas with consistent wind patterns. The transition to renewable energy is not only beneficial for the environment but also offers economic advantages, including job creation in green technologies. However, challenges such as initial costs and the need for infrastructure development remain. In conclusion, the shift towards renewable energy is essential for a sustainable future, and continued investment and innovation will be key to overcoming current obstacles.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What is one of the key benefits of renewable energy mentioned in the passage?

options.B: It is always available regardless of location.
options.C: It eliminates the need for all energy infrastructure.
options.D: It is cheaper than fossil fuels at all times.

questions.explanation: The passage states that renewable energy helps reduce greenhouse gas emissions, making option A correct.

questions.text: Wind energy is the most effective form of renewable energy in all regions.
questions.explanation: The passage indicates that wind energy is effective only in areas with consistent wind patterns, therefore the statement is false.

questions.text: The passage suggests that renewable energy can create jobs.
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the transition to renewable energy offers economic advantages, including job creation.

questions.instruction: Match the technology to its energy source.
questions.text: Match the renewable energy technology with its respective energy source.

questions.instruction: Complete the sentence with NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS.
questions.text: The initial costs of renewable energy can be a challenge to its _____ .
questions.explanation: The passage discusses the challenge of initial costs in the transition to renewable energy, indicating that adoption is the correct term.

tactics:
Hướng dẫn

To effectively tackle this passage, focus on the main ideas presented in each paragraph. Look for keywords in the questions that can guide you back to the relevant sections of the text. Pay attention to the benefits and challenges of renewable energy as these are frequently asked topics. Summarizing each paragraph in your own words can also help reinforce your understanding of the content.
